concepts.finding:
Relevance score: One immediate opportunity involves replacing our cosine
similarity-based embedding aggregation with an additional linear layer that learns
elementwise weights while computing the dot product.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Conclusion
Redesigning our early ﬁltering system from scratch improved our ad relevance
models and system performance while simplifying our ranking stack.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
As our business has
expanded, we’ve faced new challenges of operating our ad server at growing scale
while maintaining reliability and improving performance.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
We do this through two
main phases: candidate selection and candidate ranking.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
The second phase, candidate ranking, ranks these ads to ﬁnd the most relevant
ones for the user.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
With the patches we introduced,
the pipeline looked like this:
We introduced two additional ﬁltering stages, which optimized for system eﬃciency
by eliminating low-value candidates earlier in the pipeline using simple heuristics.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
We refer to this entire stack, consisting of the two heuristic ﬁlters and the ML-based
light ranking model, as “early ﬁltering.”
However, using less robust heuristics inevitably led to some high-value ad
candidates being incorrectly eliminated before the heavy ranking stage.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
While this approach was eﬃcient
and scalable, we could not experiment with state-of-the-art model architectures or
novel loss functions.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Last year, we shipped multiple improvements to these early ﬁltering stages which
signiﬁcantly improved ad relevance.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
Modeling Improvements
Using Lolly, our in-house ML framework, for the light ranking stage, we were limited
to a very small set of supported models (primarily linear and logistic regression).
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
We concluded that an embedding-based architecture was
ideal for our light ranking use case as well.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
The user and ad embeddings are then learned jointly using a
logistic loss function, with labels obtained directly from the downstream heavy
ranking model.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
System Improvements
At serving time, the SplitNet model requires us to compute a user embedding and
an ad embedding, and generates a score for the user/ad pair calculated as the dot
product of these embeddings.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Therefore, we compute the user embedding in parallel with
candidate selection, creating no additional latency to the overall system.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Whenever we ﬁnd an ad candidate without an embedding in the
cache, we compute and store it so that the embedding is immediately available for
the next request that requires it
At ranking time, we just need to fetch ad embeddings from the cache and calculate
the dot product with the user embedding th
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
As a
result, computing the dot product is signiﬁcantly faster than the previous logistic
regression computation, and so the SplitNet model decreased CPU utilization in our
serving system by 20%:
The system performance gain allowed us to remove one of the heuristic ﬁlters in our
early ﬁltering stack,
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation
